text: I find myself in a world of glass and straight lines, observed by others yet separated from them. As I step into the room, vibrant colors and words fill the space, yet I feel a quiet tension within—my actions and intentions swirl in ambiguity. The bright, open environment highlights my sense of exposure and awkwardness. There’s a longing for understanding: I ponder the chasm between what I intend, what is seen, and how we are all just grasping for connection in these public spaces. I feel vulnerable, but also awakened to the nuances of perception and interaction.
